whatItMeasures: Magnesium is a mineral involved in over 300 enzymatic reactions in the body, including energy production, protein synthesis, muscle contraction, nerve function, and blood glucose control; serum magnesium reflects the small fraction of total body magnesium in the blood.
causesOfHigh: Kidney failure (most common cause — kidneys cannot excrete excess magnesium)
Excessive magnesium supplementation or antacid use
Use of magnesium-containing laxatives or medications
causesOfLow: Poor dietary intake (common in Western diets low in nuts, seeds, and leafy greens)
Chronic alcohol use (increases urinary magnesium loss)
Type 2 diabetes (osmotic diuresis from high blood sugar depletes magnesium)
Diuretic medications (thiazides and loop diuretics increase urinary loss)
Chronic kidney disease with tubular loss
causesOfLowSource: NIH Office of Dietary Supplements. Magnesium Fact Sheet. https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Magnesium-HealthProfessional/
symptomsSource: NIH Office of Dietary Supplements. Magnesium. https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Magnesium-HealthProfessional/
